message:
[MEDIUM] fcp-core: cap merge_crdt_states branches at 4 MiB each

merge_crdt_states previously handed both `state_a` and `state_b` straight
to `ciborium::from_reader` with no bounds. Each branch is untrusted CBOR
coming from remote connector-state objects, and the function deserializes
BOTH branches before any check — so an attacker with write access to the
mesh-gossiped state object can two-sidedly amplify a crafted payload into
allocation pressure.

Introduces MAX_CRDT_STATE_BYTES = 4 MiB — large enough for realistic
multi-thousand-entry LwwMap/OrSet states, tight enough to reject
pathological ones. Both branches are length-checked before either is
decoded.

Tests: oversized branch_a and branch_b each produce a
CrdtMergeError::Deserialization whose message names which branch was
rejected — this keeps operator logs actionable when a peer sends garbage
(the caller can tell which neighbor's state blew the cap).

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-core --lib merge_crdt_states_rejects →
3 passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
